title: Miles to Kilometers Converter
shortDescription: Convert miles to kilometers with exact decimal results and clear examples.
metaDescription: Convert miles to kilometers using a precise factor, with decimal results, worked examples, and clear unit interpretation.
intro: Enter miles and get the equivalent distance in kilometers immediately.
overview:
Miles and kilometers measure the same kind of distance, but they belong to different unit systems. This converter uses the standard factor so you can turn a road distance, running distance, or map measurement into kilometers without doing the math by hand.

The result is shown as a decimal value, which is useful when you need a precise distance for planning, logging, or comparison. If your input includes a fractional mile, the tool preserves that precision in the converted result.
howItWorks: The tool multiplies the value by the standard mile-to-kilometer factor.
Read the kilometer result, including decimals when needed.

useCases.title: Road trip planning
useCases.description: Convert a highway distance in miles to kilometers when comparing route lengths across countries.

useCases.title: Running and cycling logs
useCases.description: Translate workout distances from miles into kilometers for training records and pace charts.

useCases.title: Map and route comparison
useCases.description: Check how far a place is in kilometers when a map or app gives the distance in miles.

examples.title: Fractional mileage
examples.description: Fractional miles are converted with full decimal precision.

examples.title: Large distance
examples.description: Useful for long road distances and route comparisons.

faq.question: What factor does this converter use?
faq.answer: It uses the standard relationship: 1 mile = 1.609344 kilometers.

faq.question: Why does the result include decimals?
faq.answer: Decimal output keeps the converted distance precise, especially for fractional miles and longer routes.

faq.question: How should I read a very small input?
faq.answer: Small mile values will convert to small kilometer values with more decimal detail, so check the full number before rounding.

faq.question: What is the most common mistake when using this tool?
faq.answer: People sometimes enter kilometers by mistake or expect the result to round automatically. Make sure the input is in miles and round only if your use case needs it.
